benchmark_protocol.baseline_runner: schema-v12/matrix-v6 runner from detached baseline revision
benchmark_protocol.optimized_runner: schema-v13/matrix-v7 runner from the recorded git revision

orders.name: optimized-then-baseline
orders.numeric_and_mixed: optimized v13 2+5 followed by baseline v12 2+5
orders.formula_hyperlink: optimized v13 1+3 followed by baseline v12 1+3

orders.name: baseline-then-optimized
orders.numeric_and_mixed: baseline v12 2+5 followed by optimized v13 2+5
orders.formula_hyperlink: baseline v12 1+3 followed by optimized v13 1+3

benchmark_protocol.source_generation: Each matrix generated one deterministic source in a separate process and reused it for every warm-up and measured run. Paired baseline and optimized source SHA-256 values were identical for all profiles and both orders.
benchmark_protocol.representative_policy: Measured run nearest total_editor_ms median; earliest run breaks ties.
benchmark_protocol.verification: Every optimized run passed schema-v13/matrix-v7 telemetry validation, output-plan byte accounting and planned raw compressed-payload equality. Every baseline run passed its schema-v12/matrix-v6 validator. Representative outputs were checked with openpyxl after benchmark timing.
benchmark_protocol.discarded_runs: An earlier preliminary baseline-then-optimized round overlapped unrelated Tina/FeatherDoc/Gradle builds and showed package/temporary-IO scheduling pollution. It is not copied into this bundle and supports no claim.

validation.focused_tests: 6/6 passed: worksheet event reader, public Patch cells, worksheet transformer, package editor c5, sheetdata guards and malformed source failures
validation.no_images_profile: build and image-disabled runtime smoke passed
validation.install_consumer_smoke: passed for production, stored-only and no-images installations; FASTXLSX_HAS_IMAGES was 1, 1 and 0

qualification: Only transform/source-scan stage medians and deterministic traffic changes that held in both execution orders are claimed. Total editor, save, temporary-file and package-writer elapsed values remain system-sensitive and are not generalized.
